phases.name: preparation
phases.note: Early setup and body orientation before the motion loads.

phases.name: load
phases.note: The coiled position just before explosive movement — where potential energy builds.

phases.name: release
phases.note: Peak velocity of the left wrist — contact or release point.

phases.name: follow_through
phases.note: Deceleration and balance recovery after release.

guide.summary: Your Forehand reaches 53% overall similarity to the pro, with 6 areas worth addressing. The biggest is your Hip (24° off). Power flows from the legs up through trunk rotation into the racket arm.
strengths: Your trunk rotation closely matches the pro (within 3°).
Your shoulder line tilt closely matches the pro (within 4°).

keyIssues.title: Hip: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your hips are more extended/upright than the pro's (you 20.53° vs pro 44.74°, about 24° less) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: standing too tall means the legs never load, so the stroke becomes all arm.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: sit into a deeper hip load during the unit turn, then drive up and through the ball. It shows up most during the release phase — focus your reps there.

keyIssues.title: Ankle: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your ankle is stiffer/less flexed than the pro's (you 98.34° vs pro 119.61°, about 22° less) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: limited ankle range reduces ground force and a stable base at contact.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: allow the shin to travel forward and lock the ankle firmly at contact. It shows up most during the preparation phase — focus your reps there.

keyIssues.title: Shoulder: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your arm stays closer to the torso than the pro's (you 128.78° vs pro 147.73°, about 18° less) — a clear gap.
keyIssues.cause: too little arm separation shrinks the swing arc and the racket-head speed it can build.
keyIssues.fix: create more space between arm and torso in the take-back to lengthen the swing arc. It shows up most during the preparation phase — focus your reps there.

keyIssues.title: Knee: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your knee is straighter than the pro's (you 25.07° vs pro 36.27°, about 11° less) — a small gap.
keyIssues.cause: stiff, straight knees skip the leg drive that powers a modern groundstroke.
keyIssues.fix: add a clear knee bend in the load, then push up off the court through contact. It shows up most during the release phase — focus your reps there.

drills: Loaded open-stance drives — coil onto the back hip and explode up into contact
Full-extension swings reaching through a long contact zone
Split-step into a loaded knee bend, then an explosive shadow swing
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up

workouts.title: Mobility & activation
workouts.focus: Free up and prime the joints flagged in your Forehand.

warmup.name: 90/90 hip switches
warmup.description: Rotate between hip positions to free the joint.

warmup.name: Knee-to-wall ankle rocks
warmup.description: Drive the knee over the toes to free the ankle.

warmup.name: Band shoulder dislocates
warmup.description: Pass a band overhead and back to open the shoulders.

main.name: Romanian deadlift
main.description: Train the hip hinge and posterior chain. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (24°).

cooldown.name: Couch stretch
cooldown.description: Open the hip flexors of each leg.

workouts.notes: Do this on training days before skill work.

workouts.title: Corrective strength
workouts.focus: Build strength where your mechanics differ most from the pro: hip (24°), ankle (22°), shoulder (18°).

warmup.name: Dynamic full-body warm-up
warmup.description: Light cardio plus the mobility drills above.

main.name: Romanian deadlift
main.description: Train the hip hinge and posterior chain. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (24°).

main.name: Single-leg calf raise
main.description: Strengthen the calf and stabilize the ankle. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (22°).

main.name: Landmine press
main.description: Build stable overhead pressing strength. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (18°).

cooldown.name: Couch stretch
cooldown.description: Open the hip flexors of each leg.

workouts.notes: 2–3× per week with a day of recovery between sessions. Push the main lifts, but end a set the moment form degrades.

workouts.title: Skill & sequencing
workouts.focus: Transfer the corrections into your tennis forehand.

warmup.name: Shadow reps
warmup.description: Slow-motion Forehand reps focusing on the cues.
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up

main.name: Loaded open-stance drives — coil onto the back hip and explode up into contact
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your hip — you're closing a measured ~24° gap to the pro.
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up

main.name: Banded ankle dorsiflexion mobilizations
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your ankle — you're closing a measured ~22° gap to the pro.

main.name: Full-extension swings reaching through a long contact zone
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your shoulder — you're closing a measured ~18° gap to the pro.

workouts.notes: Pay attention to the preparation → load → release → follow through sequence.
